reasoning: The most recent value for the AAA corporate spread index on Dec 5 is 0.33%, having fallen from 0.37% in mid-November. Wait, since Dec 7 is a weekend, the reference value will effectively be this 0.33% from Friday, Dec 5. Over the last week, the spread has demonstrated zero momentum, remaining flat at 0.33% for five consecutive trading days. Because the resolution requires a strict increase (>0.33), any "flat" or "no change" scenario resolves to NO, which heavily suppresses the YES probability over short time horizons. Over the 1-week horizon (Dec 14), it is more likely to stay flat or drift slightly lower toward its Q3 2025 floor of 0.30-0.31%, hence a probability below 0.5 (I estimate ~0.41). As the horizon expands to 1 month (Jan 6) and 3 months (Mar 7), the probability of mean reversion increases. Since 0.33% is very near the historical floor, any long-term drift is structurally biased to the upside. Assuming a gradual widening back toward normal levels (like the 0.40% seen earlier in 2025), the probability of exceeding 0.33% crosses the 0.50 threshold by March 2026. Therefore, probabilities shift progressively closer to 0.5 or slightly above as uncertainty dominates and mean-reverting upward drift comes into play.
